mnemonics: FAM Directs Cache Mapping: Fully Associative, Associative, Direct-mapped
keyPoints: The three mapping functions are Direct-mapped, Fully Associative, and Set-Associative.
The 'cost' of a memory system includes the physical size, monetary price, and access time (latency).
A larger cache size generally decreases the miss rate but increases the cost and access time.
commonMistakes: Confusing block size (line size) with cache size. Cache size = number of blocks * block size.
Assuming a direct-mapped cache has no replacement policy. A new block always replaces the old one in its specific line.
Thinking a larger cache always results in better performance. After a certain point, the increased hit time can negate the benefits of a lower miss rate.
